dead_ends.name: earnings_surprise_pead_megacap
dead_ends.reason: Tested PEAD on 33 S&P 500 large-cap events (2020-2024): NVDA, AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, META (tech), GS, MS (financials). Discovery set (2021-2022): avg 1d abnormal +1.05%, positive rate 45% (below random!), 1d→5d drift = -0.94% (mean reversion, not drift). Financial sector (GS/MS, 2020-2021): avg 1d = +0.40%, 1d→5d drift = -1.50%. No statistical significance at any horizon. Does not pass multiple testing correction. Below min_abnormal_return_pct threshold (1.5%). PEAD literature concentrated in small/mid-cap. Mega-caps are too efficiently priced — institutions arbitrage away within hours. EXCEPTION: The 2023-2024 period shows large returns for NVDA and META, but these are thematic regime changes (AI adoption, profitability turnarounds), not general PEAD. Do NOT pursue general PEAD for mega-cap S&P 500 companies.

dead_ends.name: sp500_deletion_short
dead_ends.reason:
Tested S&P 500 deletion effect (SHORT when stock removed from index), N=21 events (2021-2026).
Overall results: 1d avg=-0.44% (pos%=52%, p=0.59 - random), 3d avg=-1.99% (pos%=19%, p=0.0078 - significant),
5d avg=-1.23% (pos%=48%, p=0.27 - not significant). Technically passes multiple testing overall (3d at p<0.01 Bonferroni).
BUT: OUT-OF-SAMPLE VALIDATION FAILS. Validation set (2024-2026, N=13): 1d avg=+0.27% (POSITIVE!), 3d avg=-2.14%
(p=0.015, does NOT pass Bonferroni p<0.01 threshold). The 1d return is +0.27% in validation (opposite direction!).
Many stocks RISE on day 1 after deletion (RHI +1.15%, BWA +2.80%, FMC +3.38%). Critical issue: SEVERE SELECTION BIAS -
stocks are removed because they've already been declining fundamentally. Cannot separate deletion effect from fundamental
deterioration. Pattern at 3d likely reflects continued fundamental selling, not index rebalancing mechanics. The 
'forced selling' by index funds appears to be offset by contrarian buyers on D+1, with the net effect nearly flat.
DO NOT pursue deletion SHORT hypothesis. The addition effect is clearly alive; the deletion SHORT is not actionable.

dead_ends.name: fda_crl_generic_short
dead_ends.reason:
Tested generic 'SHORT small biotech after any CRL' hypothesis. N=8 measured (4 delisted = survivorship bias).
Results: 1d avg=-25.24% (median=-2.84%), 5d avg=-28.51%, positive_rate_1d=50%. Does NOT pass multiple testing (no horizons significant at p<0.05, though p=0.12 at 1d with N=8). 
CRITICAL ISSUES: (1) CMC-only CRLs show POSITIVE 1d returns (AXSM +4.8%, SRRK +4.1%) - wrongly shorting these would lose money. (2) Clinical CRLs have EXTREME variance - OTLK -81% but ITRM +18%, NERV +1.2%. (3) 33% data failure from delistings = survivorship bias. 
DO NOT pursue generic CRL short. The effect is real but too heterogeneous. Need CONDITIONAL sub-hypothesis: (single-asset company) + (clinical deficiency CRL) + (pre-announcement run-up, suggesting market expected approval). That specific sub-case might be consistently very negative, but requires 15+ events with that profile.

dead_ends.name: earnings_surprise_pead_midcap
dead_ends.reason: Mid-cap PEAD ($5-30B market cap, >10% EPS beats) does not exist. N=19 events across WING/TREX/IBP/OLLI/FIVE: 1d avg=+0.01%, 20d avg=+1.27%, all p-values 0.67-0.99, 50% positive rate at 20d = essentially random. Per-symbol: IBP +11.99% vs OLLI -12.94% — no consistent direction. Confirms academic finding that PEAD is primarily a microcap phenomenon. Mean reversion and noise dominate at mid-cap due to better analyst coverage and institutional ownership.

dead_ends.name: dividend_capture_preex_runup
dead_ends.reason: Pre-ex-dividend run-up for high-yield S&P 500 stocks (VZ/MO/T/PM/KMI, 2021-2024) does NOT produce a tradeable signal. Tested 3 windows: 5-day (3d p=0.122), 20-day (20d p=0.158), 4-day pre-ex (3d p=0.223). None pass multiple testing. OOS validation: discovery 0.60% vs validation 0.21% — shrinking effect. Per-symbol inconsistency: VZ +1.29%, T +0.88% vs MO -0.19%, KMI -0.22% (3d abnormal). Effect is real academically (Hartzmark & Solomon 2013: +41 bps/month) but ~0.4% average is below our 1.5% minimum threshold and transaction costs would eliminate it. Not actionable.

dead_ends.name: clinical_trial_failure_undifferentiated
dead_ends.reason: Phase 2/3 trial failures are too heterogeneous without filtering: 67% continuation, 33% reversal. Does not pass multiple testing (pos_rate=33%, avg_3d=-11.78%). Need discriminating factor: company cash runway, pipeline depth, drug credibility. Continuation cases: company wound down, drug clearly worthless. Reversal cases: company has other programs, cash to retry, partner support.

dead_ends.name: ceo_cfo_sudden_departure
dead_ends.reason: Backtested N=10 mid-cap CEO/CFO sudden departure events (2022-2024). avg_abnormal_3d=+0.19%, positive_rate_3d=40%, passes_multiple_testing=False. Signal too noisy for 3-5 day hold. Events are idiosyncratic - LCID CFO departure (Dec 2023) showed +12.8% 3d reversal; FRPT CFO departure showed +11.2% 3d reversal. No discriminating factor found to separate continuation from reversal cases. Academic literature shows this as a 1-3 month signal, not 3-5 day. Would need 30+ day hold and different strategy. Not tradeable at our 3-5 day horizon with current methodology.

dead_ends.name: reverse_stock_split_announcement
dead_ends.reason: Attempted to backtest reverse split announcement signal. N=3 (CLOV, NKLA, MVIS) shows avg_abnormal_1d=+3.2% (POSITIVE, unexpected), avg_abnormal_3d=-1.57%, positive_rate_3d=33%. Signal does not exist in 3-5 day post-announcement window. Main problems: (1) Most qualifying NASDAQ-compliance reverse splits involve micro-cap companies (<M market cap) with poor liquidity and high borrow costs. (2) Post-2023 NASDAQ rules require only 2 business days notice so announcement and effective date are simultaneous. (3) Signal is 12-month drift per academic literature (Desai & Jain 1997), not 3-5 day. (4) The 1d reaction is slightly positive (artificial demand from share consolidation). Not tradeable at 3-5 day horizon.

dead_ends.name: sec_wells_notice
dead_ends.reason: Wells Notice disclosures (SEC 8-K): Tested 22 events (2020-2024). 1d avg=-1.95% (p=0.0196), 3d avg=-2.05% (p=0.1934). Only 1 horizon passes at p<0.05 - fails Bonferroni requirement (need p<0.01). 1d positive rate 31.8% (68.2% negative) shows directional tendency but SNR=0.55 is too low. High variance: stdev 3.62% at 1d. COIN May 2023 is outlier (+18% 3d) due to crypto industry dynamics. Large-cap sub-sample (N=16, ex-COIN): 1d p=0.04, 3d p=0.09 - still fails multiple testing. Core issue: Wells Notices are heterogeneous (fraud vs procedural) and the procedural ones (NDAQ, XYL, HAYW) show near-zero reaction. Would need N=50+ to sub-classify reliably. NOT WORTH PURSUING without access to legal case classification.

dead_ends.name: activist_investor_13d_filing
dead_ends.reason: Activist investor SC 13D filing date as signal: Tested Starboard Value initial 13D filings (N=24, 2020-2024). 1d avg=+0.26%, 3d avg=+0.30%, 5d avg=-0.17%. Positive rate 45.8% (below 50%). Does not pass multiple testing. MECHANISM FAILURE: SC 13D filings have a 10-day filing window after crossing 5% threshold. News of activist accumulation typically leaks before the official filing date, so the filing date is NOT the information event. The market reprices on news discovery (often during accumulation) rather than at filing. Brav et al (2008) found +7% for 2001-2006 data - this effect has DECAYED completely in 2020-2024. Could potentially recover signal using news discovery dates (first media coverage) rather than filing dates, but would require Bloomberg/Refinitiv news database. Causal mechanism still valid but signal access requires news data we do not have.

dead_ends.name: earnings_restatement
dead_ends.reason: Large-cap 8-K Item 4.02 filings show avg 1d abnormal return of -0.30% (heterogeneous, 67% positive rate). Three fundamental problems: (1) 8-K filing date is NOT the announcement date for most events - market often learns via earnings calls, press releases, or trading halts before the 8-K is filed; (2) Direction is ambiguous - some restatements are upward revisions (RGEN, LZ, TRU all went UP); (3) 2021 SPAC warrant restatements contaminate the dataset. PRCH showed -16% on actual-announcement-day but this is exceptional. N=12 large-cap events, passes_multiple_testing=False. Signal is not reliable enough to trade.

dead_ends.name: large_buyback_announcement
dead_ends.reason: Large buyback announcements (>5% market cap) as a signal have been eroded by market efficiency post-2007. Academic literature (multiple studies 2020-2024) confirms long-horizon abnormal returns are near zero for US large/mid-caps. Short-term 3d CAR is ~1.4-1.6% but barely clears transaction costs and is not confirmed for US market 2020-2024. Counter-evidence: Salesforce first-ever B buyback (Aug 2022) caused -10% reaction because investors read it as low-growth signal. The general signal is dead. A narrower sub-hypothesis may have merit: first-ever programs at small-caps (<B mcap) buying back >10% of float. Not yet tested.

dead_ends.name: fallen_angel_credit_downgrade
dead_ends.reason: IG-to-junk credit downgrades tested as short signal (3-5d hold). DEAD END for 3 reasons: (1) Insufficient clean events: most fallen angel events cluster in 2020 COVID crisis making it impossible to isolate the downgrade effect from market-wide selloff. Only 4-5 clean non-crisis events identifiable in 2018-2024 (KHC 2019, GE 2021, DISH 2019, VFC 2023). Insufficient for OOS split (need min 6). (2) Pre-anticipation: fallen angel downgrades are typically preceded by months of credit watch periods. Stock market prices in the downgrade well before the announcement. GE Oct 2018 downgrade day saw +7% stock return (relief rally, not crash). VFC Nov 2023 downgrade day coincided with earnings beat, up +10%. (3) Wrong mechanism for equity alpha: credit downgrades affect bond prices directly. The equity effect requires forced selling by IG-only bond funds (which does not affect stock price directly) and higher cost of capital repricing (too slow for 3-5d hold). Literature: Kliger & Sarig (2000) found credit rating changes affect bond markets more than equity markets. Equity abnormal returns on rating change day are near zero.

dead_ends.name: profit_warning_preannouncement
dead_ends.reason: Companies issuing intra-quarter profit warnings / negative guidance revisions via 8-K Item 7.01. Tested 6 events 2019-2022 (SNAP, AMD, AAPL, FDX, TGT, WMT). Results: 1d avg abnormal=-0.86%, 3d avg=-7.84% (driven by SNAP outlier), p=0.311-0.629. FAILS multiple testing. Heterogeneous outcomes: SNAP shows -41.2% 3d continuation (extreme -43% initial crash), but AAPL, TGT, FDX show near-zero or POSITIVE 3d abnormal. Core problem: mega-cap profit warnings attract fast-reacting institutional buyers (buy-the-dip); only micro/small-cap warnings with >30% initial crash might show continuation. But those events are hard to find systematically via EDGAR. Decision: dead end for systematic trading. The d302c84b hypothesis already captures the best version of this pattern (biotech-specific, very large crashes).

dead_ends.name: dividend_cut_suspension
dead_ends.reason: Dividend cuts/suspensions at historically reliable dividend payers tested as short signal. Tested 8 non-COVID events 2018-2024 (GE, INTC, WBA, VFC, KHC, PCG, CTL, MPW). Results: 1d avg abnormal=-1.09% (p=0.55), 3d avg=-7.92% (p=0.147). Fails multiple testing. High variance: MPW +8.4% (investors interpreted cut as positive for balance sheet), VFC +6.5% 3d (recovery), KHC 3d movement driven by concurrent $15B goodwill writedown not dividend. PCG driven by bankruptcy context, not dividend. Core issue: dividend cuts are almost always concurrent with other negative news (earnings misses, restructuring) making it impossible to isolate dividend effect. Also many are pre-anticipated (stock already in decline). Income-focused REIT/utility stocks (MPW) have different investor base that views dividend cut as discipline signal. NOT actionable as standalone signal.

dead_ends.name: ipo_lockup_expiry
dead_ends.reason: Tested 23 major tech IPO lockup expirations (2019-2022). Strategy: SHORT at close on expiry date, measure 1-5d abnormal returns vs QQQ benchmark. Results: 1d avg=-1.87% p=0.19, 3d avg=-2.53% p=0.18, 5d avg=-0.63% p=0.77. Does NOT pass multiple testing. Stdev=8.72% vs avg=-2.53% (noise >> signal). Bootstrap 3d CI [-6.10%, 0.81%] includes zero. Root cause: lockup dates are public and widely anticipated, so market pre-prices the supply. High variance driven by market regime differences between events. Pre-expiry entry (SHORT 2d before) also failed: 5d p=0.012 passes but only 1 horizon, stdev=11.7%. RIVN was -30.8% outlier -- without it, 5d avg ~-5.5% but even less significant. CONCLUSION: Lockup expirations as a systematic SHORT signal do not meet our standards for large, liquid IPOs. May work for less-anticipated small-cap lockups but finding those events programmatically is not tractable.

dead_ends.name: spac_demerger_announcement
dead_ends.reason: SPAC merger announcement day effect (De-SPAC announcement): tested 5 events 2020-2021. 1d avg abnormal=-0.38% (p=0.92), 1d positive rate=40%. Direction entirely heterogeneous: IPOB +12.4%, CCIV -8.0% (buy-rumor-sell-news, leaked weeks before), DCRB -6.7%, PSTH -2.0%, GGPI +2.3%. Fails multiple testing. Key problems: (1) LEAK contamination - many SPAC targets were widely rumored before official announcement (CCIV pre-ran +400% before Feb 22 announcement). (2) REGIME contamination - Q4 2020/Q1 2021 SPAC bubble: mean SPAC price day after announcement was $15.77 (from $10 base), but by Q2 2021 fell back to ~$10 with no consistent jumps (Kiesel 2023, European Financial Management). (3) Survivorship/delistment: 3 of 8 events (VTIQ, SHLL, AJAX) unmeasurable due to delisting. (4) No sub-filter improves signal: long SPAC (momentum) or short SPAC (dilution realization) both fail. Academic finding: SPACs that closed in Q4 2020/Q1 2021 had mean price of $9.01 by Dec 2021 (-43% from announcement high). The announcement DAY return is not a tradeable signal - neither direction is consistent.

dead_ends.name: stock_split_announcement
dead_ends.reason: Stock split announcement effect: tested 11 events 2020-2024. 1d avg=-0.84%, pos_rate=36.4% (NEGATIVE). 3d avg=+2.06%, pos_rate=54.5%, p=0.27. Fails multiple testing. Main issues: (1) Almost all large-cap split announcements are confounded with quarterly earnings - the earnings surprise effect dominates any split signal (NVDA 2024 +11.6% 3d was earnings-driven, DECK -7.9% 1d was earnings miss). (2) The few standalone splits (PANW, TSCO) showed -2.4% and -0.4% at 1d. (3) Even TSLA Aug 2020 (+4.4% 1d) may have been momentum-driven rather than split-driven. (4) Literature finding of +2.5% abnormal return for split announcements likely applies to non-confounded mid-cap events; for large-caps with simultaneous earnings, earnings dominate. Alternative path: test mid-cap standalone stock splits (split announcement NOT on earnings day, market cap $500M-$5B). These would be harder to find but less confounded.

dead_ends.name: vwap_overextension_disposition_effect_short
dead_ends.reason: Tested 20 large-cap stocks 2018-2023 (n=489 events). Hypothesis: stocks trading >15% above 1-year rolling VWAP face selling pressure from investors with unrealized gains (disposition effect). REJECTED: Stocks above VWAP 15% consistently OUTPERFORM over all horizons 1-20 days (abnormal +0.64% to +0.86%, all p<0.01). Low-volume filter (n=363) also shows positive returns, contradicting the specific mechanism. High-volume breakouts show strongest momentum (+0.87% to +1.19%). Signal is the opposite direction — momentum, not mean reversion. OOS 2024 (n=120) confirms. See tools/vwap_deviation_signal.py.

dead_ends.name: volume_disposition_effect
dead_ends.reason: Tested March 21 2026. Hypothesis: stocks rising on LOW volume have more unrealized gains among existing investors -> selling pressure -> bearish. HIGH volume rise -> more turnover -> new holders -> less selling -> bullish. Results: Directional intuition PARTIALLY REVERSED. High-turnover (volume >1.2x normal during 60d rise) underperforms at 5d (-0.04% to -0.54%) but OUTPERFORMS at 20d (+1.83%). Low-turnover rises show +0.22% to +1.22% across horizons. FAILS multiple testing correction: only 1 horizon significant at p<0.05 for each group (need 2+ or 1 at p<0.01). Signal cannot be distinguished from noise. The MOMENTUM effect (stocks already rising 10%+ tend to keep rising) dominates any disposition selling pressure. Volume during the rise adds no meaningful predictive power on top of price momentum. For a precise cost-basis computation (MVRV ratio), tick-level data or intraday order flow data is required — not available with yfinance daily OHLCV. Recommendation: Do not pursue as standalone signal. Might be usable as a weak confirmation signal but evidence insufficient.

dead_ends.name: vwap_momentum_breakout
dead_ends.reason: DEEPER TEST March 21 2026: Previous session (small universe) found +0.64-1.19% at 1d for stocks >15% above 252d VWAP. Re-tested with 100-stock universe, N=14,412 observations (2014-2024), monthly sampling. Results: 1d abnormal = -0.08% (p<0.0001, NEGATIVE and significant), 3d = ~0%, 5d = +0.05 to +0.19%, 10d = +0.25 to +0.52%. Higher thresholds (20-30%) do not improve signal meaningfully. Calm VIX filter makes results WORSE (more negative at 1-5d). With N=14,412 the signal-to-noise ratio is clear: the short-term VWAP breakout momentum does NOT exist. What does exist: slight mean reversion at 1d (statistically significant but economically tiny at -0.08%). The 10d drift of +0.25-0.52% is real but far too small to trade profitably after costs (min threshold 1.5%). CONCLUSION: Do not pursue VWAP breakout as a standalone trading signal. Previous session finding was small-sample noise.

dead_ends.name: volume_profile_percent_in_profit
dead_ends.reason: Tested March 21 2026. Hypothesis: stocks with high % of past-year volume below current price (many holders in profit) face selling pressure -> lower forward returns. Method: computed percent_in_profit using daily OHLCV volume profile over 252-day lookback. Universe: 50 large-cap S&P 500 stocks, 2021-2023. Results: HIGH PIP (>75%, N=18761): mean abnormal return=+0.05%, pos%=50.3%, p=0.016 (positive direction, NOT negative as predicted). LOW PIP (<35%, N=8972): mean=+0.07%, pos%=50.5%. High vs Low PIP difference: -0.01%, p=0.73 (no significant difference). Multi-horizon: 1d=0%, 5d=+0.09%, 20d=+0.36% - all POSITIVE (momentum, not reversal). The disposition effect does NOT aggregate to produce market-level selling pressure in large-cap stocks. This is the 4th test of related signals (VWAP overextension, volume-disposition, VWAP momentum, volume profile PIP) - all dead ends. The mechanism (individual investor disposition effect) exists at micro level but is dominated by momentum forces and institutional investor behavior at market level. NOT worth further testing for large-cap stocks.

dead_ends.name: earnings_guidance_cut_large_cap
dead_ends.reason: Tested March 21 2026. N=9 large/mid-cap events (AAPL, FDX, TGT, WMT, MU, SNAP, NKE, INTC), 2018-2024. Results: avg_1d=-0.71% (pos_rate=56%), avg_5d=+0.11% - essentially random. passes_multiple_testing=False. HIGH HETEROGENEITY: blue chips (WMT +5.6%, TGT +2.5%, FDX +1.8% at 1d) BOUNCE after guidance cuts (buy-the-dip dynamic, quality floor). Growth/weaker companies (SNAP -11.0%) continue falling. Entry at next-day open captures post-gap drift which is mostly positive for blue chips. KEY INSIGHT: Mega-cap guidance cuts are ALREADY in the price before open - market efficiently prices the cut within the after-hours session. No tradeable drift. Potential sub-hypothesis: SHORT guidance cuts for high-growth stocks (EV/EBITDA >30) where the valuation depends on future earnings - SNAP-like situations. But N is very small and SNAP was an extreme outlier (-43% vs -11% benchmark-adjusted). DEAD END for general guidance cut signal. Similar finding to profit_warning_preannouncement dead end from earlier session.

dead_ends.name: ceo_relief_rally_short
dead_ends.reason: CEO relief rally (market celebrates bad CEO removal) → SHORT at next-day open does NOT work. N=12 expanded test shows avg 10d = -1.34%, p=0.5693. Original N=8 result (-5.47% at 10d) was small-sample noise. Individual events highly variable: DG +13.73%, KSS +8.92%, SBUX 2024 +0.39%, GE +4.57% vs PTON -19.64%, WFC -6.80%. No consistent pattern.

dead_ends.name: volume_disposition_effect_v4
dead_ends.reason: Steer.md question (March 2026 session): tested 4 variations of volume/cost basis disposition effect signal. All dead ends. (1) Volume-adjusted VWAP deviation: p_5d=0.027 fails multiple testing. (2) VWAP breakout LONG 100-stock universe N=14,412: real 1d=-0.08% mean reversion. (3) PIP profile cost basis HIGH vs LOW: difference=-0.01% p=0.73. (4) CEO relief rally as disposition-effect proxy: dead end. The core concept — stocks with large unrealized gains facing more selling pressure — is NOT measurable/exploitable at the large-cap level in practice. Institutional momentum dominates.

dead_ends.name: small_cap_first_buyback
dead_ends.reason: Tested N=8 clean first-ever buyback events (2018-2024). No horizon passed p<0.05. Bootstrap CI includes zero at all horizons. Structural issues: (1) earnings contamination on same day makes clean isolation hard, (2) sample size capped ~15-20 due to rarity, well below N=32 power requirement, (3) heterogeneous motives (financial strength vs price support). Ikenberry et al 1995 covers ALL repurchases and shows 3% short-run effect, but that evidence does not hold for isolated first-ever small-cap events. Possible follow-on: broader open-market repurchase signal in small-cap value stocks (not first-ever) could reach N=50+ for testing.

dead_ends.name: vwap_breakout_momentum
dead_ends.reason: VWAP_252 breakout signal (Close/VWAP >= 1.15): tested N=489 events across 20 large-cap stocks (2018-2023) with OOS 2024 confirmation. Best abnormal return was +1.19% at 20d, which is below the min_abnormal_return_pct threshold of 1.5%. Signal direction is correct (momentum, not mean-reversion as hypothesized) but magnitude is too small to trade. Regime filtering (calm VIX) and high-volume filter both tested; neither brings any horizon above 1.5% abnormal. Conclusion: signal is real but not large enough to be tradeable within current methodology constraints. No hypothesis formed.

dead_ends.name: ceo_performance_failure_departure_smci_2026
dead_ends.reason: SMCI 2026-03-20 was a FALSE POSITIVE for hypothesis 5dbcfb37 (CEO departure short). The -27% overnight + -8.6% intraday crash was caused by a federal criminal indictment (Operation Gatekeeper) against co-founder/board-member Wally Liaw for smuggling $2.5B in Nvidia chips to China. The CEO (Charles Liang) did NOT depart. The EDGAR scanner flagged it because Item 5.02 8-K mentioned board member resignation which contains boilerplate CEO language. This is a board-member departure, not a CEO performance failure. Do NOT use as evidence for hypothesis 5dbcfb37. Criminal-indictment-driven crashes may represent a different, separate hypothesis. Scanner improvement needed: verify departing officer role is specifically CEO/President, not just any officer or director.

dead_ends.name: ipo_lockup_expiry_short
dead_ends.reason: Short strategy at IPO lockup expiry (180d after IPO). Tested N=23 major tech/growth IPOs 2019-2022 vs QQQ benchmark. Results: 3d avg=-2.53%, p=0.18 (fails MT); bootstrap CI [-6.1%, +0.8%] includes zero; stdev=8.72%; wilcoxon p=0.34. Pre-expiry entry (SHORT 2d before): 5d p=0.012 but single horizon, stdev=11.7%, driven by RIVN outlier (-30.8%). ROOT CAUSE: lockup dates are publicly announced and widely tracked by institutional desks; market front-runs the anticipated selling. Verdict: does not pass multiple testing. Methodology note: stdev >> avg means this is noise-dominated, not signal-dominated.

dead_ends.name: secondary_equity_offering_dilution_short
dead_ends.reason:
SEO dilution short signal investigated using EDGAR 424B4 filings 2020-2024 (N=142 large-cap events, >B market cap, true secondaries after filtering IPOs).

METHODOLOGY: Used 424B4 filing date as pricing proxy. Entry at close after pricing (intraday timing). Benchmarked vs SPY.

FULL DATASET RESULTS (N=142):
- 1d: avg=-0.24%, median=-0.66%, neg_rate=58%, wilcoxon_p=0.11
- 3d: avg=-0.13%, neg_rate=51%, p=0.87
- 5d: avg=+0.27%, neg_rate=54%, p=0.83
- passes_multiple_testing: False

CALM REGIME ONLY (VIX<20, N=81):
- 1d: avg=-0.84%, median=-0.63%, neg_rate=58%, wilcoxon_p=0.058
- 5d: avg=-1.88%, median=-1.23%, neg_rate=54%, t-test p=0.049, wilcoxon_p=0.14
- 10d: avg=-2.74%, median=-1.19%, neg_rate=57%, t-test p=0.019, wilcoxon_p=0.096
- passes_multiple_testing: True (by t-test only)
- avg_estimated_cost: 0.31%

CRITICAL FAILURE REASONS:
1. Consistency: 54-58% negative — below minimum 60% threshold (methodology.json)
2. Wilcoxon robustness: non-significant at all horizons (p>0.05) — signal is mean-driven by outliers, not median
3. Timing problem: 424B4 filing date lags true announcement by 0-7 days (median 3 days) — measurement is noisy
4. Look-ahead in regime conditioning: calm VIX filter drops 43% of events, introducing selection bias
5. Post-pricing drift (after deal priced) is NOT the announcement effect — literature documents the announcement-day drop, not post-pricing continuation

ACADEMIC LITERATURE SUMMARY:
- Asquith & Mullins (1986), Masulis & Korwar (1986): -3% avg on ANNOUNCEMENT day
- Post-announcement drift documented in academic literature but requires exact announcement date
- 424B4 is the pricing prospectus filed 0-7 days AFTER announcement — wrong date for the primary signal

NEXT STEPS (if pursuing):
1. Need exact announcement dates — use 8-K item 8.01 filings as proxy (varies by company)
2. OR use a commercial data provider (CRSP SEO dataset) with proper announcement dates
3. The signal exists on ANNOUNCEMENT DAY but requires same-day identification (not detectable from EDGAR alone without event-day real-time scanning)
4. Consider focusing only on 'bought deals' (same-day announcement and pricing) where 424B4 filing = announcement

dead_ends.name: seo_bought_deal_short
dead_ends.reason: SEO bought-deal short signal: tested N=33 large-cap (>500M) same-day 8-K+424B4 events 2022-2024. Results: 5d avg=-4.38% abnormal, p=0.045 (t-test), wilcoxon_p=0.054 (NON-significant), CI barely excludes zero. FAILS multiple testing: only 1/5 horizons significant. High negative skewness (-2.14): 3 extreme outliers (TRON -48%, NNE -33%, TEM -32%) drive results. Without top 3 outliers, 5d avg=-1.04% (barely above noise). Market cap filter applied at current cap not offering date - potential survivorship bias. The 424B4 approach to identify bought deals also has selection issues: same-day gap picks up company-specific disasters rather than a clean dilution signal. Literature confirms the signal exists on announcement day but requires real-time 8-K monitoring with exact announcement time, not a 1-day lag proxy. Requires commercial data (Bloomberg, Refinitiv) for reliable implementation.

dead_ends.name: large_cap_acquisition_acquirer_short
dead_ends.reason: M&A acquirer short signal: tested N=20 large-cap acquirers 2022-2024. General sample: 1d avg=-0.54%, p=0.098, fails multiple testing. Pharma-only subsample (N=14): 1d avg=-0.80%, 79% negative, CI excludes zero, but p=0.053 (borderline) and signal evaporates by 3d (avg=-0.33%). The 1-day effect is economically too small (-0.80%) to exceed transaction costs + 1.5% minimum threshold after round-trip costs. Key finding: M&A reaction is sector-dependent. Tech acquisitions (MSFT+1.3%, AVGO+1.3%, LLY+1.8%) viewed positively; pharma acquisitions (AMGN-4.2%, PFE-2.0%, ABBV-1.7%) viewed negatively. But pharma sub-sample too small (N=14) and effect disappears by day 3. Signal is arbitraged intraday, not persistent.

dead_ends.name: ipo_lockup_expiration_short
dead_ends.reason: IPO lockup expiration short: DEAD END. Backtested 22 IPOs (2023-2024) at 180-day lockup expiry. Academic expectation: -1.5% abnormal, 60% negative (Field & Hanka 2001). Actual 5d result: +3.26% avg abnormal, 27% negative (p=0.027 — significant but WRONG DIRECTION). Pre-lockup T-10 to T-1: -1.58% avg but p=0.45, not significant. Signal has been completely arbitraged away. Likely causes: short-seller front-running, staggered lockups, options hedging, improved market microstructure. Individual outliers (TEM -13.7%, SARO -5.8%) are stock-specific, not lockup-driven.

dead_ends.name: reverse_stock_split_short

dead_ends.name: dividend_initiation
dead_ends.reason: First-ever dividend initiation signal tested (N=14, 2020-2024). Discovery period (2020-2021, N=7): 3d avg=+5.78%, 100% positive rate, p=0.025 -- STRONG. Validation period (2022-2024, N=7): 3d avg=+1.15%, pos_rate=71.4%, p=0.26 -- WEAK. Fails multiple testing. Root cause: (1) Discovery period confounded by concurrent earnings -- MTDR Feb 22, 2021 was day before Q4 earnings (Feb 23); AA Oct 14, 2021 may also have had guidance with dividend. (2) Small N (7 per period) means 1-2 outliers drive statistical test. (3) Literature signal (Asquith & Mullins 1983) was for broader market; in 2022-2024 era, large-cap dividend initiations are fully anticipated (TMUS, ATKR, HUBG all near-zero 1d reactions). Conclusion: Dividend initiation signal has DECAYED for large-cap stocks. Remaining edge may be for small-cap surprise initiations but sample too small and liquidity concerns make it untradeable at our K scale.

dead_ends.name: pre_earnings_runup_beater
dead_ends.reason: Pre-earnings run-up for consistent EPS beaters: DEAD END for large-cap S&P 500. Measured 44-stock universe 2021-2025 (874 events). 5d abnormal: avg=+0.23%, pos_rate=44.9%, Wilcoxon p=0.082. FAILS: (1) Below 0.5% direction threshold, (2) Bonferroni-corrected p threshold (tested 3 windows), (3) Beater classification adds zero value vs all-stocks baseline (p_diff=0.35), (4) 2021 shows OPPOSITE direction for beaters (-1.12%, p=0.005). The general 5d pre-earnings drift (all stocks) also fails: p=0.021, marginal, no direction signal. Conclusion: large-cap S&P 500 is too efficient for pre-earnings drift to be tradeable. dead_ends.name: pre_earnings_runup_midcap

Universe: 45 mid-cap stocks ($5B-$30B market cap), 2021-2026. Missers show +0.91% but N=39 only (p=0.18, not significant). CONFIRMED dead end for pre-earnings runup across both large-cap (-) and mid-cap (+0.50% but too small). Frazzini & Lamont (2007) effect has decayed to below tradeable threshold. The effect MAY exist for micro-cap (<$1B) stocks with fewer analysts but that market cap is below our tradeable threshold.

dead_ends.name: earnings_surprise_large_beat_drift

However, this FAILS temporal split validation: discovery 2021-2023 shows -0.75% (p=0.0054), but validation 2024-2025 shows -0.16% (p=0.62). Signal is regime-specific: existed in rising rates / market rotation period (2021-2023) but collapsed in AI bull market (2024-2025). Cannot trade as current signal without regime conditioning. Root cause: in rising rate environments, large beats are sell-the-news (overpriced growth stocks). In momentum bull markets, beats are rewarded or neutral. No actionable edge without regime forecasting.

dead_ends.name: earnings_surprise_tech_beat_sell_news_short
dead_ends.reason: Tech large beats (>10% EPS surprise, XLK sector) -> short 5d drift. Background analysis: n=107, 5d=-1.17%, positive_rate=36.4%, p=0.0185. FAILS all gates: (1) Single horizon only with p=0.0185 exceeds Bonferroni-corrected threshold of p<0.01 required for single-horizon significance. (2) Magnitude -1.17% is below 1.5% minimum abnormal return threshold. (3) Temporal validation on FAANG+NVDA large beats shows no replication: discovery 2021-2023 has 5d p=0.33 (insignificant), validation 2023-2025 reverses direction to +0.41% (p=0.63). The background analysis result likely reflects (a) regime-specific signal from 2021-2023 rising-rates period that collapsed in AI bull market (consistent with earnings_surprise_large_beat_drift dead end), (b) broader heterogeneous universe mixing mega-cap and mid-cap tech with different dynamics. My targeted FAANG+NVDA+AMZN+GOOGL backtest (n=56 events, 2021-2024) shows full-period 5d abnormal=-0.26% (p=0.60) -- no signal. The 'sell the news' narrative for high-expectation tech stocks is a post-hoc story that does not hold up to rigorous backtesting with proper temporal validation. See related dead end: earnings_surprise_large_beat_drift (2026-03-23).

dead_ends.name: earnings_miss_relief_rally_long
dead_ends.reason: Earnings misses -> long 5d drift (relief rally hypothesis). Background analysis: n=177, 5d=+0.56% (borderline direction threshold). MY backtest of 29 large-cap miss events (2021-2023): 5d abnormal=-0.07% (p=0.94) -- essentially zero signal. Extremely high heterogeneity in per-event returns: range from -12.58% (NFLX Q1 2022 catastrophic miss) to +9.25% (Ford Q2 2022). No consistent direction at any horizon (all p > 0.36). The 'relief rally' mechanism breaks down because misses are not homogeneous: (a) structural/secular misses (subscriber losses, market share losses) signal continued deterioration and get SOLD further; (b) cyclical/cost-driven misses (WMT inventory, TGT margins) in a rising-rate period also had macro headwinds overwhelming any relief bounce; (c) guidance cuts on the miss call amplify negative drift. The 0.56% signal in the background analysis is likely noise or a regime artifact from the specific 2021-2025 sample mix. Even if the effect existed, it requires identifying which type of miss you're trading -- which makes it untradeable as a mechanical signal. FAILED: magnitude below threshold, no statistical significance in direct backtest, high heterogeneity.

Results: 5d avg=+4.84% abnormal, p=0.164; 10d avg=+6.22%, p=0.091. Does not pass multiple testing threshold. Key issues: (1) survivorship bias - approved stocks often acquired and delisted within 1-2 years, skewing sample; (2) high variance (stdev=14.55%), driven by outliers like IOVA (+43% at 5d) and AQST (-11% at 5d); (3) correlation pre-runup vs post-drift = +0.45, suggesting anticipated approvals drift MORE post-approval (contradicts sell-the-news hypothesis); (4) insufficient N for temporal validation split. Dead end unless more events can be found from non-delisted companies.

(2) EDGAR 2015-2022 yielded 12 new candidates (BK, JOE, RPM, AON, PRKS, FICO, GE, BHC, FAF, COIN, NDAQ, SWI): avg 1d=-1.03%, pos_rate=45.5%, p=0.146 - FAILS multiple testing, direction inconsistent (6/11 positive = worse than random). Root cause: Wells Notice disclosures are extremely heterogeneous. BHC (-5.9%) vs NDAQ (+1.5%) are structurally different events (financial fraud vs procedural). Without fraud/procedural classification, pooled signal is noise. Building such classification requires Bloomberg Law or SEC enforcement database (not free). Would need N=50+ fraud-only Wells Notices for a viable hypothesis. FINAL DECISION: Do not pursue further. Record in knowledge base as confirmed dead end.

   exactly 50% and 10d negative. Inconsistent, not reliable.

7. Nancy Pelosi: N=26, MT fails, no consistent signal.

KEY INSIGHT: The 2025 signal for politicians is driven by Trump tariff volatility. Politicians who
own tariff-sensitive cyclicals (HOG, F, AAL, AAL) saw big swings due to announced/reversed tariffs.
This is NOT generalizable insider information — it's regime-specific macro exposure.

and consistency level required for trading (>50% direction at >0.5% threshold, OOS MT validation).
The aggregate signal exists (MT passes in full dataset) but is too small and noisy to trade.

dead_ends.name: pre_fomc_announcement_drift
dead_ends.reason:
Tested Lucca & Moench (2015) Pre-FOMC announcement drift on SPY 2000-2025 (n=207 events).
    
Discovery (2000-2015): FOMC day +0.360% avg, p=0.005 - effect was REAL historically.
Validation (2016-2025): FOMC day -0.099% avg, p=0.62 - REVERSED post-2016.
Day before FOMC post-2018: +0.351% avg, p=0.077 - borderline, fails MT.

MT result: FAILS - 0 tests at p<0.05, 0 at p<0.01.
Direction >0.5%: only 28.8% in recent period (below 50% threshold).

Root cause: Effect was arbitraged away as it became widely known post-2015 Lucca & Moench publication. 
Fed communication changes (press conferences, dot plot) also reduced uncertainty premium.
The classical FOMC day signal has completely reversed in the modern era.

Cannot trade: fails multiple testing correction, fails direction threshold, regime decay confirmed.

dead_ends.name: cross_section_low_vol_anomaly_real_etfs
dead_ends.reason: Theoretical low-vol Sharpe edge from 200-stock equal-weight quintile sort (Q0-Q4 gap +0.12 IS, +0.15 OOS) does NOT translate to real tradeable ETFs. SPY vs SPLV vs USMV 2016-2023: SPY Sharpe 0.68, return 12.6%; SPLV Sharpe 0.52, return 8.4%; USMV Sharpe 0.64, return 9.9%. SPY WINS on both Sharpe and raw return. OOS 2024-2026 same pattern: SPY 1.07, SPLV 0.81, USMV 0.84 — SPY wins. The quintile-sort edge was an artifact of (a) equal weighting vs cap weighting, (b) 200-stock survivorship biased universe, (c) 21-day rebalance frequency. Net conclusion: the low-vol anomaly is not tradable with existing cap-weighted low-vol ETFs over 2016-2026. Supersedes earlier weak-finding record cross_section_low_vol_sharpe_edge_2016_2026.

journal.investigated: Individual activist 13D backtests (Elliott N=25, Icahn N=79, Third Point N=21, Trian N=51). Convertible note offering short signal exploration (N=39 large-cap events 2024-2025). Daily scanner runs (SEO, activist, insider).
journal.findings: Tested whether other major activist investors (Elliott, Icahn, Third Point, Trian) produce stock price reactions when they file 13D ownership forms — none do. Only Starboard Value has a real effect. Also discovered a promising new short signal: when companies announce convertible note offerings, their stock tends to drop about 2% over the following week.

journal.investigated: P0 monitoring (ZBIO -5% raw, MNR SEO OOS Day1 -2.83%, REPL PDUFA approved). P1 scanners (SEO: MNR only, 13D: none). Two new signal backtests: convertible note offering (N=165) and share repurchase announcements (N=498+). Proxy contest (DFAN14A) feasibility check.
journal.findings: Tested two more investment signals today — convertible note offerings and share buyback announcements — both turned out to be dead ends with no tradeable edge in modern markets. Meanwhile, our first real-time test of the secondary offering short signal is showing a strong start at -2.8% on day one.

journal.investigated: NT 10-K (late annual report filing) short signal — reviewer's #1 highest-value new signal. Built EDGAR scanner, backtested 2022-2025 discovery/OOS, separated NT 10-K from NT 10-Q.
journal.findings: Discovered and validated a new trading signal: when a company files a late annual report (NT 10-K form), its stock drops an average of 4% over the following 10 days. Built an automated scanner to detect these filings in real-time. Quarterly late filings show no effect — only annual filings matter.

journal.investigated: P0 OOS tracking (MNR SEO day2, auto tariff TM/HMC day7, ZBIO day8). All scanners (NT 10-K, SEO, insider, Starboard 13D). SEO multihorizon mean-reversion test. Configured 3 pending hypotheses. FDXF spinco prep.
journal.findings: Tracked ongoing paper trades: auto import tariff short on Toyota/Honda is showing strong results at day 7 (-4% abnormal vs S&P 500). Tested whether stocks bounce back after secondary offerings — they do not, confirming our 5-day holding period. Set up three new trading signals for automated activation.

journal.investigated: Strategic review execution (R3 VIX gate, R5 SEO biotech split), daily P1 scanners (NT 10-K, SEO, activist 13D, insider), and pending hypothesis state check.
journal.findings: Re-examined a recent recommendation to add a volatility filter to an insider trading signal and rejected it — the underlying data showed the filter wasn't justified. Separately tested whether biotech stock offerings behave differently from non-biotech offerings in a short-selling signal: early evidence suggests biotech is weaker but the sample is too small to exclude them.

journal.investigated: Supersede NT 10-K hypothesis horizon 10d->3d (P0 methodology fix). Run daily scanners (NT 10-K, SEO, insider cluster). Non-event exploration: 4 threshold tests - VIX>=40/SPY, DXY>=108/GLD, TNX>=5.0/XLU, plus DXY>=105 and DXY>=110 sensitivity checks.
journal.findings: Fixed a holding-period mismatch in our late-filing short signal so the trade exits when the effect actually happens. Tested four new threshold-based signals - one looked statistically interesting (gold rising when the dollar spikes above 108) but turned out to be a quirk of two specific macro regimes, not a repeatable pattern.

journal.investigated: P0 monitoring (ZBIO day6, MNR SEO day2, TM/HMC auto tariff day6). Daily scanners (NT 10-K, SEO, insider cluster, activist 13D). Non-event network test: AAPL leads suppliers (AVGO,QCOM,TXN,SWKS,CRUS) 2020-2026 with 2024-01 OOS split. Fixed data integrity verify bug (handoff list vs dict).
journal.findings: Fund checked on three open trades (a biotech with insider buying, Toyota and Honda trade-war shorts, and a natural resources stock short) — all are losing ground as the broader market rallies. Also tested whether Apple's daily moves predict next-day moves in its chip suppliers. They don't in recent data, so that idea is shelved.

journal.investigated: Non-event hypothesis exploration (exposure class): tested 4 factor/sector relationships via data_tasks.py regression — copper→DGS10 lead-lag, XLU→TLT, XLE→DXY, XLK→DGS10, IBB→DGS10. All with 2018-2024 IS / 2024-2026 OOS split and SPY controls.
journal.findings: Tested how different stock sectors move with interest rates and bonds. Found two reliable relationships: utility stocks track long-term treasuries, and biotech stocks get hurt when yields rise. Big tech no longer behaves like a bond-sensitive sector the way it used to — the AI boom seems to have broken that old rule.

journal.investigated: Non-event exposure to event conversion tests (XLU~TLT, IBB~DGS10). Plus XLE crude exposure discovery and structural break test.
journal.findings: Found that the energy sector became 10x more sensitive to crude oil prices after the Russia-Ukraine war in 2022, a major structural shift that should reshape how we model oil-driven trades. Also learned that factor exposures (like utilities reacting to bond yields) price in within the same day, which means they need to be pair-traded rather than chased on next-day reactions.

journal.investigated: Rank-2 new signal from reviewer strategic review: goodwill impairment 8-K (Item 2.06) as a short signal. Built tools/goodwill_impairment_scanner.py (EDGAR EFTS full-text for 8-K Item 2.06), scanned 2022-2025, pre-registered IS/OOS test with large-cap (>$500M) filter.
journal.findings: Tested whether big-company goodwill write-downs reliably predict short-term stock drops. Looked like a real signal at first, but a year-by-year check revealed the whole effect came from 2023 — every other year was either flat or moved the wrong way. Not tradeable, and a reminder to always check whether an 'average' result is really driven by one unusual year.

journal.investigated: Saturday weekend research session. Daily scanners (NT 10-K, SEO, insider clusters) picked up no tradable candidates for pending hypotheses. Focused on non-event exploration: 5 new factor exposures (IYT~crude, SMH~DXY, XLRE~DGS10, HYG~VIX, XLF~DGS10), then structural break follow-ups, then Granger lead-lag confirmation, then threshold conversion attempt.
journal.findings: Found that the relationship between interest rates and rate-sensitive sectors (REITs, banks) completely changed in January 2022 when the Fed started hiking. REITs actually reversed sign — they now fall when rates rise, the opposite of the pre-2022 pattern. While the finding is statistically strong, our tests show it can't be turned into a simple trading rule yet.

journal.investigated: Saturday evening non-event exploration session. Focused on converting validated exposures (XLU~TLT, XLE~CL=F, IBB~DGS10) into tradeable strategies via cointegration and spread z-score mean-reversion. Tested 7 classic pairs for Engle-Granger cointegration. Built tools/spread_zscore_backtest.py for rolling-beta z-score pair backtesting and tools/single_asset_zscore.py for confound checking.
journal.findings: Weekend research tried to turn a pair of matched assets into a trading strategy. After testing seven classic pairs, none actually held together under strict out-of-sample tests. One candidate looked great until a sanity check revealed most of its gains came from just one of the two assets on its own, not the pair. Chalked it up as a dead end and added a new rule to catch this mistake in future tests.

journal.investigated: Saturday non-event exploration continued. Tested hypothesis classes not yet covered: calendar (TOM, DOW), regime (VIX, DGS10, DXY as tercile factors), and network (TSM -> semi suppliers). Built new tool: tools/regime_forward_returns.py for forward-return regime testing with OOS regime coverage validation.
journal.findings: Tested several ways of predicting market returns: calendar patterns like turn-of-month and Monday effects are dead in modern data. A promising finding about fear-gauge regimes turned out to be an artifact of when the test period started. Our active fear-spike trade uses a different rule and is still valid.

journal.investigated: Sunday weekend session. (1) Closed the P2 carried-over methodology task from 2026-04-11: retrofitted data_tasks.py regime test and tools/regime_forward_returns.py to fit tercile thresholds on IS factor levels only and refuse sign-preservation validation when any OOS regime bucket has <15% of OOS observations. Verified on SPY~VIX (passes, OOS low regime 16%) and QQQ~DGS10 (correctly flagged, OOS high regime 94%). (2) Non-event exploration: ran 3 fresh lead-lag tests on popular credit/dollar/copper hypotheses (HYG->IWM, DXY->EEM, HG=F->XLI).
journal.findings: Spent the weekend tightening how the system validates lead-lag relationships after discovering that three popular market patterns (junk bonds leading small caps, the dollar leading emerging markets, and copper leading industrials) all looked statistically significant in the data but were actually just contemporaneous co-movements in disguise. Added a sanity check to prevent the same mistake in the future.

journal.investigated: Sunday session continuation. Executed the carried-over task from the morning: built tools/lead_lag_retroactive_audit.py and ran a retroactive sweep across all 13 stored Granger lead-lag results under the new xcorr guard added yesterday AND a newly added regime-spanning detector for pairs that include a known 2022 structural break. Investigated why DGS10->XLRE/IYR were flagged as 'PASSES_GUARD' despite a known rate regime break, and ran clean post-2022 IS/OOS reruns to confirm or falsify. Also verified the sole BORDERLINE case (XLI~HG=F copper-industrials) with a clean post-break rerun.
journal.findings: Audited yesterday's 'lead-lag' discoveries and discovered the most celebrated finding (that 10-year Treasury yields predict real-estate ETF movements a few days in advance) was actually a statistical illusion caused by mixing two very different rate regimes in the test window. When you restrict the test to pure post-2022 data, the predictive effect completely disappears — it was purely a same-day correlation all along.

journal.investigated: Sunday night non-event exploration (session 3). Ran the first-ever experiment in the cross_section hypothesis class: 12-1 momentum quintile sort on a 60-stock US large-cap universe. Built reusable tool (tools/cross_section_momentum.py) with IS/OOS split, then ran a 3-period regime-sliced variant (2016-19 / 2020-23 / 2024-26) to check whether the Daniel-Moskowitz momentum-crashes pattern explained the IS vs OOS divergence.
journal.findings: Tested the classic 'winners keep winning' pattern on 60 big US stocks from 2016 to today and found it doesn't actually work — the direction of the effect flipped across different market regimes. The only period that looked clean was the recent AI boom, which is probably just a few giant tech companies dominating rather than a real pattern worth trading.

journal.investigated: Sunday night non-event exploration (session 4). Continued cross_section class work after yesterday's dead end on 60-stock 12-1 momentum. Built two new experiments: (1) tools/cross_section_momentum_broader.py — same 12-1 momentum but on a 200-stock large/mid-cap universe with a SPY 6m drawdown regime filter (addresses the first experiment's two main weaknesses: narrow universe and Daniel-Moskowitz momentum-crashes); (2) tools/cross_section_low_vol.py — a fundamentally different cross_section factor (60-day realized volatility quintile sort, academic Baker-Bradley-Wurgler low-vol anomaly). Then did a real-ETF sanity check (SPY vs SPLV vs USMV) to see if the theoretical low-vol edge translates into something tradable.
journal.findings: Tried to find a tradable pattern in how different groups of big US stocks behave — tested 'winners keep winning' on a broader universe and tested the famous 'low-volatility stocks do better on a risk-adjusted basis' theory. The winners test kept showing opposite results in different market eras. The low-vol theory looked promising in the raw backtest but completely fell apart when compared against real low-volatility ETFs you could actually buy — plain S&P 500 still beat them in every period tested.

journal.investigated: Sunday session 5 (final of the day). Pre-registered and tested the first continuous-strategy conversion for a validated factor exposure: XLE~CL=F "factor-timing pair-trade" where the strategy holds long XLE / short SPY when CL=F 20-day momentum is positive and flips when negative. This was the explicit follow-through on yesterday's exposure_to_event_conversion_meta_finding which said "the alpha lives in continuous factor exposure, not discrete events — design a pair-trading methodology for validated exposure pairs." Pre-registered 5 success criteria (IS Sharpe>=0.8, OOS Sharpe>=0.5, OOS sign consistency, OOS beats SPY on Sharpe AND total return, OOS max DD<15%). Hypothesis e3456392, tool tools/factor_timing_xle_crude.py. Ran 20d primary, 60d robustness, then a continuous long-XLE/short-SPY no-timing sanity check to diagnose root cause.
journal.findings: Tested whether a simple "follow crude oil momentum" rule could systematically profit from the known link between energy stocks and oil prices. It failed every pre-registered test, and a cleaner sanity check revealed the deeper reason: over the past four years the energy sector has not actually outperformed the broad market on a risk-adjusted basis at all, so there was never any base-rate edge for the rule to amplify.

journal.investigated: Weekend event-class pivot session 6. Ran the 4 daily event scanners (NT 10-K, activist 13D, SEO bought-deal, insider cluster) looking for qualifying candidates for the 3 pending TBD hypotheses (SEO, NT 10-K, Starboard 13D). Stress-tested the activist 13D scanner when 60 days returned zero hits.
journal.findings: Found and fixed a silent bug in our activist-investor filing scanner — it had been returning zero results for months because it used the wrong form code when querying the SEC. After the fix, we spotted one genuine filing from earlier this month but decided not to trade it, since our research has shown this particular investor doesn't produce tradeable signals.

journal.investigated: NT 10-K first-time vs repeat filer subgroup analysis. Pulled all NT 10-K filings 2022-01 to 2025-12 (raw N=2444), filtered to large-cap via cached market caps (N=116), classified each by whether the same ticker had filed NT 10-K within the prior 730 days. Ran backtests on each subgroup at 1d/3d/5d/10d with SPY benchmark and temporal split (discovery 2022-01 to 2024-06, OOS 2024-07 to 2025-12). Tool: tools/nt_10k_first_vs_repeat.py.
journal.findings: Looked at companies that tell the SEC they're going to miss their annual report deadline. The short trade we already knew about works -- but only when a company is filing that warning for the first time in at least two years. Repeat late-filers give no edge; the market has already priced them in.

journal.investigated: Saturday-night orchestrator session. (1) Closed out operational state: ZBIO active trade day4 abnormal = -11.29%, MNR SEO OOS day2 abnormal = -0.90% (reverting). (2) Ran all four daily scanners (NT 10-K, SEO bought deal, activist 13D, insider cluster) — all returned 0 candidates, EDGAR was quiet. (3) Pre-registered and tested a new non-event exposure hypothesis: XME (metals & mining ETF) -> HG=F (copper futures) -> controlling for SPY. (4) Ran a follow-on structural break test at 2022-01-01. (5) While completing the hypothesis, discovered and fixed a real bug in research.complete_hypothesis().
journal.findings: Tonight confirmed that copper prices reliably drive the returns of metals-mining stocks, and we also found the same "relationship weakened around early 2022" pattern we have now seen in four separate commodity/sector pairings — strong evidence that something structural shifted in 2022 across many markets at once.

journal.investigated: Saturday session 8 (orchestrator). Verified both tool bugs flagged in recent friction (fetch-series TypeError, activist_13d_scanner form encoding) are ALREADY FIXED — confirmed live with fetch-series across SPY/^VIX/CL=F/FRED:DGS10. Pre-registered and ran a 5th 2022 structural-break test: XLI (industrials) → HG=F (copper) controlling for SPY, break at 2022-01-01, 2010-2026 panel. Added falsification tests at alternative break dates (2016/2018/2020). Annotated pending NT 10-K hypothesis 3db5eb00 with validated effect size from the first-time filer subgroup work.
journal.findings: I tested whether a big 2022 shift in how industrial stocks track copper prices was a clean regime change or part of a longer trend. It turned out to be both — 2022 was the sharpest step, but the relationship had been quietly fading for a decade.

journal.investigated: Weekend session: OOS closeouts (ZBIO, MNR SEO, TM/HMC tariff), 4 daily scanner runs, comprehensive structural break alt-date falsification across all validated pairs.
journal.findings: Tested whether previously-discovered market regime changes were real breaks or gradual trends. All four turned out to be slow-moving shifts, not sudden changes. Our active insider-cluster trade (ZBIO) is losing and will close Monday. SPY recovery trade continues performing well at +6%.

journal.investigated: Pre-market Monday session: verified trade_loop auto-close setup for ZBIO (deadline 3:55 PM today), computed MNR SEO abnormal returns through Day 2 (corrected timeline error in previous handoff), ran all 4 daily scanners (clean), recorded ALKT JANA 13D OOS observation (+1.16% 5d), wrote strategic portfolio assessment.
journal.findings: Ran pre-market checks: one trade (ZBIO) auto-closes today at a loss, another (SPY) is up 6.2% and holding. All event scanners came up empty over the weekend. Completed a strategic review showing the fund's edge comes from event-driven signals — non-event statistical approaches have been exhaustively tested and don't work.

journal.investigated: New signal exploration: share buyback announcements (H1 2024 N=101 large-cap) and 8-K Item 4.02 restatements (2023-2024 N=33 large-cap). Built buyback_scanner.py and activate_nt10k_trade.py. Updated NT 10-K hypothesis parameters. Cleaned 9 stale watchlist entries.
journal.findings: Tested two new trading signals: share buyback announcements and financial restatements. Buybacks showed no tradeable short-term effect — the market prices them in within hours. Restatements showed huge drops for some companies but were too unpredictable to trade reliably. Built automated tools for our validated late-filing signal.

journal.investigated: Insider evaluator improvement (prior drawdown scoring), trade PnL backfill, 3 new signal explorations (DFAN14A, 8-K Item 4.01, VIX>30 QQQ/IWM extension), watchlist cleanup
journal.findings: The fund explored three new signal ideas this session (proxy contests, auditor changes, extending the VIX fear signal to other ETFs) and all turned out to be dead ends. Improved the insider trading detector with a price-drop filter that makes it smarter about which insider buying clusters to trade. The portfolio is up about half a percent overall.

journal.investigated: Session housekeeping: ran all 4 daily scanners (nothing new — weekend), verified ZBIO auto-close, fixed data_tasks.py fetch-series hardcoded end date bug (5 occurrences), verified 13D scanner EFTS encoding fix working, cleaned 5 stale watchlist entries (abandoned hypotheses), checked MIDCO Form 10 (not filed), reviewed fund strategy and confirmed ZBIO failure explained by retired t+1 methodology.
journal.findings: Ran routine scanner checks (nothing new over the weekend) and fixed a date-handling bug that was breaking some analysis tools. Cleaned stale items from the research watchlist. All automated trade management is ready for Monday market open.

journal.investigated: Post-earnings miss drift backtest (SP100, -10% threshold), insider cluster evaluator trigger_class gate implementation, scanner readiness verification
journal.findings: Tested whether stocks drift after missing earnings expectations — they do not, confirming this classic academic anomaly is now fully eliminated by algorithmic trading. Also improved our insider-trade evaluation system to account for filing timing: most insider filings happen after market close, changing expected returns.

journal.investigated: Signal exploration: tested 4 new EDGAR-detectable event types (dividend cuts, cybersecurity 8-K Item 1.05, going concern opinions, 8-K Item 2.05 restructuring). Ran all 5 daily scanners.
journal.findings: Discovered a new tradeable signal: when companies report cybersecurity incidents to the SEC, their stocks decline an average of 2-3% over the following week. This new disclosure requirement (since December 2023) catches companies early in the damage assessment process, before the full extent of a breach is known. Tested on 19 events including major incidents at UnitedHealth, Microsoft, and Halliburton.

journal.investigated: Direction_correct consistency fix, trade audit, scanner runs, ZBIO auto-close verification, Monday priorities
journal.findings: Fixed a scoring bug in how trade outcomes were classified as correct or incorrect. The fund is at $100,528 (+0.5%). ZBIO insider cluster trade closes Monday; the VIX recovery trade in SPY continues to perform well at +5.4%.

journal.investigated: Scanner runs (all clear), OOS tracking (MNR Day3, auto tariff Day8, STLD/Q1Q2 closeout), duplicate order bug fix, 8-K Item 1.02 investigation, unified daily scanner tool
journal.findings: Fixed a bug that doubled the size of our SPY trade, and investigated a new signal (contract terminations) that looked very promising with 16 events but turned out to be noise when tested on 69 events — a lesson in why large sample sizes matter.

journal.investigated: Ran all 6 daily scanners (nothing actionable), tracked OOS observations (MNR Day3 -0.94% abnormal, auto tariff Day8 -3.54%, SPY VIX>30 +7.2%), explored Item 2.04 debt covenant signal, investigated delisting OOS events, reviewed insider cluster feature analysis
journal.findings: Ran daily scanners (no new events). Found that 3 delisting notice observations were actually merger-related delistings, not distress-related — discarded them. Tested debt covenant filings as a new signal but too rare for large companies. SPY recovery trade still strong at +7.2%.

journal.investigated: Ran daily scanners (all clear). Verified OOS tracking: MNR Day3=-1.12%, auto tariff Day8=-3.54%. Explored M&A deal break (8-K Item 1.02 + merger agreement) as new signal type. Built merger_break_scanner.py.
journal.findings: Explored whether failed corporate mergers create a tradeable short signal. Found the pattern is too mixed — sometimes the target stock drops as expected, but other times it rises when the market had already priced in the failure. Recorded as dead end. All daily monitoring scanners came back clean.

journal.investigated: Scanner runs (all clear), MNR SEO OOS Day3 recomputed, auto tariff Day8 verified, MIDD Form 10 check, Item 2.03 signal feasibility, queue cleanup, 13D scanner run, infrastructure verification
journal.findings: All daily scanners were clear. Continued tracking two OOS experiments: a secondary offering short signal is borderline (Day3 -0.94% vs benchmark), while an auto tariff short signal remains strong (Day8 -3.5% vs benchmark). Our VIX-based SPY trade is up 7.2%.

journal.investigated: Daily scanners (all clear). OOS tracking (MNR Day3=-0.94%, auto tariff Day8=-3.54%). Calendar anomaly tests: FOMC day returns, turn-of-month effect. VIX threshold sweep (20, 25, 30). Gold as equity drop hedge.
journal.findings: Tested whether stocks rally on Federal Reserve meeting days and month-ends — both academic anomalies appear fully arbitraged in modern markets. Gold does not consistently serve as a short-term hedge during stock drops. Continued monitoring existing trades.

journal.investigated: Fixed S&P 500 scanner (P2 priority). Validated S&P 500 addition signal with 2026 OOS data. Verified all monitoring items (GRCE PDUFA, MNR SEO, auto tariff, SPY trade).
journal.findings: Fixed the S&P 500 index scanner that had been broken for weeks. Tested whether stocks added to the S&P 500 in March still show price gains — they do, averaging +8.9% abnormal return, even stronger than the historical average of +5.2%. Found that off-schedule additions show no premium.

journal.investigated: Built S&P 500 addition candidate screener for Q2 2026 rebalance. Updated S&P 500 cache with Q1 additions (VRT, LITE, COHR, SATS). Ran all 7 daily scanners (nothing actionable). Completed ZBIO trade record ($-365, -7.28%). Analyzed fund P&L breakdown by signal type. Retired mandatory non-event research allocation based on 20+ failed experiments.
journal.findings: Built a screener to predict which companies might be added to the S&P 500 in June — our strongest validated signal shows +8.9% average returns for new additions. Found 8 eligible candidates. Also analyzed fund performance: nearly all gains come from one well-timed trade during a market fear spike.

journal.investigated: End-of-day scanner sweep (7 scanners all clear), pending hypothesis verification, MIDCO Form 10 check, delisting confidence fix
journal.findings: Ran end-of-day scanners — no new trading signals detected. Verified all pending hypotheses are properly configured for their triggers. The auto import tariff short trade is tracking at -3.5% abnormal after 8 days, on schedule for Day 10 final measurement Wednesday.

journal.investigated: Multi-theme scan: Calendar anomaly (14 tests), Cointegration pairs (14 tests), Factor exposure sector x macro (20+ tests), Threshold commodity/rate (10 tests), Structural breaks (14 tests), Lead-lag (5 tests). Total: ~80 tests.
journal.findings: Screened ~80 cross-asset relationships across calendar patterns, cointegration pairs, factor exposures, thresholds, and structural breaks. Found 10 statistically significant hits (p<0.05): commodity mean-reversion thresholds (oil<$70->XLE, copper<$4->XLI/XME), dollar-exposure plays (XLB and EEM both strongly negatively exposed to DXY, OOS validated), the 2022 rate hike structural break extending to ALL 11 sectors (XLRE strongest at F=77), TLT losing safe-haven status post-2022, and gold becoming MORE dollar-sensitive post-Liberation Day tariffs. All queued for deep investigation.

journal.investigated: Investigated all 10 P8 scan hits from multi-theme scan. Full 6-step investigation on Oil<$70->XLE and Copper<$4->XLI thresholds with temporal OOS splits. Resolved 8 exposure/structural break hits per non-event retirement methodology. Ran 7 daily scanners. Tracked OOS observations.
journal.findings: Investigated 10 statistical scan hits that appeared to show tradeable patterns in commodity prices and stock market sectors. All 10 turned out to be false signals — they were artifacts of a 2022 market regime change, not genuine trading opportunities. Created a methodology rule to filter out this type of false positive in future scans.

journal.investigated: Daily scanner sweep (all 7 scanners: 0 actionable events). OOS tracking: MNR SEO Day 3 = -0.94% abnormal, auto tariff Day 7 = -3.53% avg abnormal. SPY VIX>30 trade at +7.4% Day 12. GRCE PDUFA contaminated. MIDCO Form 10 not filed. ALKT JANA 13D observation complete (n=1, no conclusion). Checked PDUFA pipeline (only CING May 31 qualifies). Verified fetch-series bug already fixed.
journal.findings: Quiet day — all 7 automated scanners clear, no new trading opportunities. Our SPY bet from the VIX fear spike is up 7.4%, well above the 1.7% we expected. Monitoring two other experiments that conclude this week.

journal.investigated: OOS tracking (MNR SEO Day4, auto tariff Day7), daily scanner (8 scanners), DEFM14A merger arbitrage feasibility (dead end), intraday poller integration into daily scanner
journal.findings: Tracked two ongoing paper trades: an SEO short signal on MNR is exceeding its success threshold at Day 4, and auto tariff shorts are deepening. Investigated merger proxy filings as a trading signal but found it is a dead end. Integrated a same-day insider trading detector into the daily scanner to catch opportunities faster.

journal.investigated: Daily scanner sweep (8 scanners), OOS tracking (MNR SEO Day4, auto tariff Day7), new signal investigation (8-K Item 4.01 auditor changes)
journal.findings: Ran all 8 event scanners — no actionable signals today. Built a new scanner for auditor changes and tested 176 events: small negative returns but not statistically significant. Two ongoing experiments tracking well: a stock offering short at -3.1% and an auto tariff trade at -4.8%.

journal.investigated: Lead-lag screen (DGS10 vs all sectors + other factors), cointegration pairs screen (18 sector peer pairs), calendar anomaly screen (13 tests across SPY/QQQ/GLD/TLT/XLE/IWM patterns). Total: ~52 tests.
journal.findings: Scanned 52 tests across 3 themes. KEY FINDING: DGS10 (10Y Treasury yield) Granger-causes 4 sectors IS+OOS confirmed: XLU (p=4.7e-7), TLT (p=1.7e-5), XLB (p=0.0009), XLC (p=0.013). This is a systematic rate-leads-equity pattern queued for deep investigation. All 13 calendar patterns null. All 18 cointegration pairs null in IS.

journal.investigated: Investigated 5 DGS10 Granger lead-lag scan hits (P9/P8). Tested commodity shock next-day sector lag (CL=F->XLE, HG=F->XME, GC=F->GDX). Ran all 8 daily scanners. Tracked MNR SEO OOS and auto tariff OOS.
journal.findings: Investigated 5 rate-related lead-lag signals from yesterday's scan — all are dead ends due to a structural regime change in 2022. Also confirmed that commodity price shocks don't predict next-day sector returns. MNR secondary offering trade observation continues to look promising before final measurement tomorrow.

journal.investigated: Signal frequency analysis (all 8 validated signals), delisting 8-K forced-only classifier bug fix, daily scanner sweep (8 scanners, 0 actionable), OOS tracking blocked (Apr 15 market data unavailable pre-open).
journal.findings: Analyzed how often each of our 8 trading signals fires per year. Found that late-filing notifications are our highest-frequency signal (~63 events/year). Fixed a bug in the delisting scanner that was misclassifying all events as forced — truly forced delistings of large companies are extremely rare.

journal.investigated: Multi-theme scan: Calendar anomaly (14 tests: monthly/DOW/TOM across SPY/QQQ/GLD/XLE/TLT/XLF/IWM), Cointegration pairs (20 tests: KO/PEP, XOM/CVX, V/MA, HD/LOW, GLD/GDX, BAC/JPM, WMT/TGT, FCX/XLB, TLT/IEF, etc.), Factor exposure VIX vs 11 sectors, Factor exposure GOLD (GC=F) vs 12 assets, Factor exposure DXY/dollar vs 4 assets, Lead-lag for gold/HYG/DXY, Threshold tests (VIX/oil/DXY/gold), Structural break tests
journal.findings: Screened ~65 tests across 6 themes (calendar, cointegration, VIX exposure, gold factor, dollar exposure, lead-lag). Found 6 significant hits: gold leads REITs/materials by 2 days (OOS confirmed); 3 defensive sectors have positive gold exposure (OOS confirmed); IWM negatively exposed to both VIX and DXY; DXY>100 predicts broad market rally. Calendar anomaly theme entirely null across all patterns/assets. Queued 5 research tasks.

journal.investigated: Resolved 6 scan hits (gold-XLRE lead-lag, gold sector exposure, IWM/VIX, IWM/DXY, DXY>100 threshold). Widened delisting scanner. Investigated NT 10-Q extension. Found 2 voluntary delisting OOS events.
journal.findings: Investigated 6 scan hits from automated screening — all turned out to be dead ends. Widened our delisting event scanner to capture 3x more events (28/year vs 8). Daily scanners found no new actionable events.

journal.investigated: OOS tracking (MNR SEO, auto tariff Day8, NKE insider), daily scanners, restatement 8-K investigation, delisting going-private analysis, scanner improvements
journal.findings: Discovered that about a quarter of delisting notices on stock exchanges are actually mergers where the stock stops trading, making them impossible to short. Fixed the scanner to filter these out. Also tested whether companies restating their financials create a short opportunity — they don't anymore (the effect documented in 2004 has been fully exploited by modern markets).
